findings.fix: Remove `expose`/`reducible` from these definitions and route consumers through the characteristic lemmas instead; add any missing lemma rather than requiring unfolding.
findings.issue: `tailFamily`, `tailProcess`, and `futureFiltration` expose their bodies (`@[reducible, expose]` at lines 28/33 and `@[expose]` at line 89), making the implementation part of the public surface even though users have `tailFamily_eq_iSup_comap`, `tailProcess_eq_iInf_tailFamily`, and `futureFiltration_apply`.

findings.evidence: Lines 37-46 define the two normal-form lemmas without attributes; only `futureFiltration_apply` at line 98 is tagged `@[simp]`.
findings.fix: Mark the appropriate normal-form restatements with `@[simp]` (or provide separate simp-normal-form lemmas if these names should remain untagged).
findings.issue: The normal-form lemmas `tailFamily_eq_iSup_comap` and `tailProcess_eq_iInf_tailFamily` are documented as normal forms but lack `@[simp]`, so the public API does not give simp the canonical expansions once the bodies are hidden.

summary: The public interface exposes implementation bodies where characteristic lemmas already exist, and its stated normal-form lemmas are not registered for simplification. These are fixable API-surface issues.
